objective: Review-only QA audit of telemetry contract, launch proof, and result-path readiness.
main_findings: Telemetry contract is implemented but not fully documented across README/operator surfaces (counter schema, warning semantics, fallback behavior).
Atomic write intent is strong, with .tmp+rename used in key paths, but some launcher state writes remain direct json.dump without rename semantics.

Monitor consumes swarm-state telemetry robustly with fallbacks/warnings, but does not read child-agents.jsonl directly and suppresses some parsing errors, reducing observability when state shape drifts.
Result and progress paths are mostly coherent, but commander bundle handoff details are under-documented and monitor visibility is tied primarily to results/*.json.
consensus_notes: All workers agree telemetry/launch-proof mechanics exist and are active.
All workers agree docs lag implementation details for telemetry schema and operator interpretation.
conflict_notes: No hard conflicts; one worker rated overall status partial for documentation/contract drift risk while others reported success with caveats.
